title: Payment method type catalogue
description: The 8 payment method types the module supports. Each type documents which providers accept it, whether saved tokens apply, and typical PCI-DSS scope. Read by PaymentMethodObserver + PaymentIntentOrchestrator when routing payments.

method_types.description: Credit / debit / prepaid card. Includes major networks (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, JCB, UnionPay). Requires 3DS2 for card transactions above the PSD2 threshold in EU regions.
method_types.typical_pci_scope: SAQ A (Academorix scope): customer confirms via provider SDK; we never see PAN. Would be SAQ D if we accepted raw card fields — deliberately avoided.

method_types.description: Direct debit from a bank account. Includes US ACH, EU SEPA Direct Debit, UK Bacs. Requires micro-deposit verification before first debit for US ACH.
method_types.typical_pci_scope: Not PCI-DSS — bank details, not card details.

method_types.description: One-time bank transfer (wire transfer, SEPA transfer). Customer initiates the transfer; provider confirms receipt. NO saved token — every transfer is a new one-time flow.
Apple Pay / Google Pay / PayPal / Venmo / Cash App. Wallet tokenizes the underlying method (card / bank / balance). Saved token stored per provider.
method_types.typical_pci_scope: Not PCI-DSS at Academorix layer — wallet handles PCI on its side.

method_types.description: Klarna / Afterpay / Affirm / Zip. Provider handles the installment agreement; Academorix sees a single capture as if it were a full-amount card payment. Provider bears default risk.
Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDC, etc. NOT supported day-1. Reserved enum for future integration via specialized providers (BitPay, Coinbase Commerce) through the CustomWebhook driver.

Cash-at-counter payment. Captured offline; recorded in the system after the fact by admin. No provider involvement — captures via CustomWebhook or direct admin entry (out-of-band from the provider intent flow).
Paper check received by mail. Similar offline flow to cash — captured via CustomWebhook or direct admin entry.

notes: The method_type enum is the authoritative list. Adding a new type = updating this catalogue + updating the PaymentMethodType enum + updating provider-capability-matrix.json.
The supported_by_providers list for each method_type is the source of truth for the PaymentMethodObserver's cross-check when creating a new saved method.

requires_customer_side_tokenization=true means the customer's SDK MUST tokenize the underlying method before hitting our API. Direct submission of raw method details is refused (PCI-DSS scope reduction).
saved_token_supported=false means the method is one-time — Payment rows may have payment_method_id=NULL for these methods, and no PaymentMethod row is stored.
